summary: Professor Joann Manson discusses the efficacy and safety of dietary supplements based on findings from large-scale clinical trials like VITAL and COSMOS. She advocates for a targeted approach to supplementation, emphasizes prioritizing a plant-based diet over pills, and warns against mega-dosing and unregulated supplement marketing.

principles.statement: Prioritize a healthy, predominantly plant-based diet before considering supplements.
principles.rationale: The majority of healthy adults can obtain necessary nutrients from a well-balanced diet. Supplements will never substitute for proper nutrition and a healthy lifestyle.

principles.statement: Take a targeted approach to supplementation rather than a generalized one.
principles.rationale: Supplement benefits occur mainly in people with specific deficiencies or health conditions (e.g., omega-3s for those with low fish intake) rather than the general population.

principles.statement: Remain skeptical of supplements without robust clinical trial evidence.
principles.rationale: Unlike pharmaceutical drugs, over 90,000 supplements exist on the market without requiring proof of safety or efficacy.

mental_models.name: Multivitamins as "Insurance"
mental_models.description: Viewing a daily multivitamin not as a replacement for a healthy diet, but as a protective baseline measure specifically to guard against age-related memory loss and cognitive decline in older adults.
mental_models.example: Taking a daily multivitamin to support cognitive health in older age, as demonstrated by the COSMOS trial.

heuristics: Only take omega-3 supplements if you consume less than 1.5 servings of fish weekly.
Look for the U.S. Pharmacopeia (USP) quality control seal when purchasing supplements to ensure content accuracy and lack of contaminants.

anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Excess amounts of dietary supplements do not provide additional health benefits and can actually be harmful.

anti_patterns.description: Using supplements as a substitute for a healthy lifestyle.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Supplements cannot replicate the complex nutritional benefits of a proper, predominantly plant-based diet.

anti_patterns.description: Assuming supplements are safe and effective just because they are on the market.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: Tens of thousands of supplements are sold without the rigorous proof of safety or efficacy that is required for pharmaceutical drugs.
